world.title: Former CJ, Mufti Endorse Tanzania’s Landmark Election Probe Commission
world.description: Former CJ Prof. Ibrahim Juma and Mufti Abubakar Zubeir have endorsed Tanzania’s new electoral commission. The move strengthens the reform process.

world.title: Nelson Mandela was a towering global symbol - but how effective was he as a president?
world.description: Nelson Mandela remains one of the most revered political leaders of modern times. He is widely credited with guiding South Africa through a peaceful transition from apartheid to democracy.

world.title: Iran reportedly to use alternative ports to bypass US blockade of Strait of Hormuz
world.description: That according to semi-official state media outlet, Mehr News Agency

world.title: Iran & Ukraine usher in the era of the underdogs in a war-torn world
world.description: Global conflicts are increasingly prolonged, with unexpected resilience from weaker forces challenging traditional military predictions. Wars in Iran, Ukraine, and Gaza exemplify this trend, where advanced tactics and adaptability allow smaller actors to sustain resistance against stronger adversaries, leading to extended engagements and higher human costs.

world.title: North Korea rapidly expanding nuclear weapons capability, UN watchdog warns
world.description: Pyongyang making ‘very serious’ progress on producing weapons, with rapid rise in activity at main nuclear complex

world.title: Why Jakarta is in a dilemma over US military overflight in Indonesian skies
world.description: Explore the complexities of US military overflight proposals in Indonesian airspace and the potential implications for regional major power rivalry. Read more at straitstimes.com.

world.title: Africa at a Crossroads: Security, Governance, and Economic Realignment
world.description: Africa faces a pivotal week of security, governance, and economic challenges, with the DRC conflict and rising inflation topping the continental agenda.

world.title: Albania’s Thriving Tourism Industry Faces Significant New Hurdles as U.S. State Department Issues Heightened Security Advisory, Urging Travelers to Exercise Increased Caution Amid Rising Concerns
world.description: The U.S. State Department urges American tourists to exercise heightened caution in Albania due to rising threats from pro-Iran groups targeting U.S. interests.

business.title: Watch US, Iran Seek Second Round of Talks & ASML Raises 2026 Sales Forecast
business.description:
Bloomberg Daybreak Europe is your essential morning viewing to stay ahead. Live from London, we set the agenda for your day, catching you up with overnight markets news from the US and Asia. And we'll tell you what matters for investors in Europe, giving you insight before trading begins.
On today's show, the US & Iran look to arrange a second round of peace talks in the coming days. President Trump tells Fox News he believes the war is "close to over." Stocks in China follow Wall Street in recouping their post-war losses on optimism for a deal.
Investors now focus on tech and earnings. Europe's most valuable company, ASML, has raised its full-year sales forecast as the surge in global AI spending boosts demand for its advanced chipmaking machines. But its improved outlook was tempered by a weaker-than-expected sales forecast in the second quarter.
Today's guest: Josie Anderson, Nomura, European Economist

business.title: Arbitration halted after Grand Falls-Windsor reaches settlement with union leader
business.description: An arbitration hearing came to an abrupt halt this week after the town and Tammie Greening reached a settlement that will see both sides “go their separate ways.”

business.title: Kerrygold-maker Ornua reports rise in turnover, profits
business.description: The maker of Kerrygold has reported an operating profit of €136.6m for last year - up 4.7% on 2024.

business.description: The UK competition regulator fines the motoring group and orders redress for 80,000 learners for breach of consumer law over fees

science.title: Graphene just defied a fundamental law of physics
science.description: In a major breakthrough, scientists have observed electrons in graphene flowing like a nearly frictionless liquid, defying a core law of physics. This exotic quantum state not only reveals new fundamental behavior but could also unlock powerful future technologies.

science.title: Scientists observed the hunting behaviour of tarantulas - and discovered something groundbreaking
science.description: Tarantulas may combine learning-based navigation and cue-based orientation to adjust their movement and hunting behaviour

science.title: Nasal Spray Reduced Brain Inflammation
science.description: Learn how an intranasal extracellular vesicle therapy reduced neuroinflammation and improved memory in aging mouse models. Read more about the study.

science.title: Launching bold ideas: Our latest Expanding Theories awardees
science.description: We are funding new Expanding Theories projects designed to spark progress in how we diagnose, monitor and treat brain tumours.

science.title: ESA begins next phase of 'fibre in the sky' optical communications project with Canada
science.description: Actionable data from space could be delivered in seconds in the future, thanks to progress towards the European Space Agency’s (ESA) faster and more secure laser communications network, HydRON. At the 41st Space Symposium in Colorado Springs, Canadian satellite communications company Kepler was awarded a contract to lead the next phase in the project’s evolution.

science.title: Returning humans to the Moon
science.description: I watched Artemis II lift off — and witnessed the first humans venture to the Moon since 1972   COMMENT | GORDON OSINSKI | Even from a distance of several kilometres, the Artemis II rocket looked huge. Then, there was a moment that felt like an eternity, as around 2,600 metric tonnes of spacecraft lifted off. …

science.title: SpaceX launches 2 batches of Starlink satellites within 20 hours
science.description: SpaceX marked a significant achievement by successfully launching two Falcon 9 rockets, carrying Starlink satellites, from Florida and California within a span of 20 hours.

science.title: The Sky Today on Wednesday, April 15: Spot the Moon and Mercury this morning
science.description: A delicate crescent Moon hangs above bright planet Mercury in the predawn sky. You may also catch Mars nearby.

ai.title: South China Morning Post
ai.description: Whoever produces tokens cheaply, at scale, has an advantage in the AI economy, just as cheap steel once decided industrial supremacy.

ai.title: AI could check millions of CT scans for heart risk. Who will pay for it?
ai.description: Millions of routine CT scans hold clues to heart disease risk. Should a new Medicare payment pathway speed the adoption of AI-based coronary calcium screening?

These initiatives aim to replace fragmented manual processes with reliable, autonomous systems, enabling organizations to boost productivity without expanding headcount.

ai.title: Revolutionizing IT: Ailoitte's AI Velocity Pods Break New Ground
ai.description: Ailoitte introduces AI Velocity Pods, a revolutionary model in software development, replacing the traditional time-based billing with outcome-focused engagements. By integrating AI with senior engineers, products are developed faster and at fixed costs, challenging the conventional IT services industry practices.
